narrative: Your loan application for 2600 Deutsche Marks to purchase a radio or TV over 18 months was carefully reviewed by our credit model, which assesses risks based on patterns from past borrowers in 1970s Germany. The model calculated a 55.5% probability that you might face challenges repaying, tipping the decision toward denial to protect both you and the bank from potential difficulties. The strongest factor pushing toward this concern was your checking account status showing less than 0 DM available, which stands out because only about 17% of approved applicants had this situation—most had some balance or none reported, signaling greater financial stability. On a more positive note, your requested amount was below the average of around 3230 DM for those approved, which helped pull the prediction toward approval by suggesting a manageable loan size. Your credit history of existing paid credits also supported you, as it matches the profile of over half of successful applicants, and your purpose of buying a radio or TV aligns well with 30% of those approved. However, these positives weren't enough to offset the primary red flag from your account status, especially combined with your relatively short time at your current residence—longer stays are more common among those approved—and a low installment rate of 4% that, while close to average, didn't fully reassure in this context. As a young foreign worker with a skilled job, 1-4 years employed, living rent-free, and no other debts or guarantors, you have many strengths that shine through, like your lack of additional installment plans and just two credits at our bank. Compared to typical approved applicants, who often have steadier savings above 100 DM or property assets, your profile highlighted higher risk in liquidity right now. To improve your chances next time, consider building up your checking account balance, extending your time at your residence if possible, or providing details on savings or assets to show stronger financial buffers. We encourage you to reapply after addressing these areas, perhaps with a smaller amount or added guarantor, as your solid job and payment history position you well for success.
